title: Amherst Island
content:
In winter, you were
        a flash of light,
        tundra against
        Arctic floor

        Warm breath
        stirred yr
        summer's breast
        and I saw
        windblown hair
        the colour of kelp
        transfix
        the lavender print
        of a scalp strewn
        shore

        Later,
        tiny bits
        from
        a calico dress
        became domiciled wings
        off butterflies,
        miniature bitterns
        ever more shadowy
        strewn across the Barrens,
        an unbridled strength from that

        Faraway isle released to orchestrate sunlight
        amongst all colonies that flower - 
        a statuesque Red Admiral,
        Banded Purple,
        feckless Comma
        all aswirl to the
        pipes of a Devil's Paintbrush,
        stranded drumfish, sage,
        and tubercular ragwort
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Cameron Brown, titled "Amherst Island", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
